skill.name: docs-retrieval
skill.description: Retrieve targeted documentation context from local ai-docs before using web search, with workflows for direct navigation, keyword discovery, multi-library gathering, and full-context loading.
skill.summary: Find relevant local documentation quickly and keep AI coding work grounded in concise source context.
skill.author: Consiliency

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution and weak-cryptography patterns, but review found them inside Markdown instructions, code fences, and documentation path examples rather than executable code. No prompt injection, secret access, data exfiltration, or malicious behavior was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Detections Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines are documentation navigation examples, inline file names, or fenced Markdown blocks showing ai-docs paths. They do not define executable scripts, shell commands, Ruby backticks, or user-controlled command execution. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: Direct review confirms the locations are prose and Markdown examples only, with no runnable code or command invocation surface in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct review confirms the locations are prose and Markdown examples only. No runnable code or command invocation surface appears in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Detections Are Text Collisions
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain documentation descriptions, path examples, table text, or keyword guidance, not MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic operations. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The suspected locations have no cryptographic API calls, hashing operations, key handling, or security-sensitive data processing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspected locations have no cryptographic API calls or hashing operations. They are documentation text and path examples.

content.value_statement: AI coding agents often waste time or context by searching broadly for library details. This skill guides them to local ai-docs first, then loads only the relevant summaries before falling back to web search.
actual_capabilities: Directs agents to check local ai-docs indexes before using web search.
Provides direct navigation steps when the library and topic are known.
Provides keyword-search steps when the relevant library or page is unclear.
Shows how to gather and consolidate context across multiple libraries.
Defines when full-context files are appropriate despite their token cost.
Gives token-budget guidance for targeted documentation loading.
limitations: Requires a local ai-docs library structure to be present in the workspace.
Does not retrieve or update documentation by itself; it instructs the agent how to read files.
May still need web search for current versions, release dates, or missing local topics.
Quality depends on the accuracy and coverage of the local documentation files.

use_cases.title: Implement Library Features
use_cases.description: Load concise API guidance, gotchas, and examples before adding code that uses external libraries.

use_cases.title: Debug Dependency Errors
use_cases.description: Find local error-handling and troubleshooting pages before spending context on broad searches.

use_cases.title: Prepare Sub-Agent Context
use_cases.description: Gather focused documentation once and pass it to sub-agents so they do not repeat navigation work.

prompt_templates.title: Find One Topic
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner lookup for a known library and topic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use docs-retrieval to find local documentation for [library] about [topic]. Load only the most relevant page summaries and list the source paths.

prompt_templates.title: Search Unknown Library
prompt_templates.scenario: Find which local library covers a concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use docs-retrieval keyword search for [concept]. Check the local library index, identify candidate libraries, then load the best matching pages.

prompt_templates.title: Gather Integration Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare documentation for work across several libraries.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use docs-retrieval to gather context for an integration involving [libraries]. Consolidate purpose, key points, gotchas, patterns, sources, and token estimate.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Retrieval Depth
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for migration or architecture work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use docs-retrieval to decide whether targeted pages or full-context files are appropriate for [task]. Explain the tradeoff and load the minimum useful context.

output_examples.input: Find retry configuration guidance for BAML.
output_examples.output: The agent checks the BAML index, finds the error-handling page, summarizes retry syntax and timeout gotchas, and cites the loaded ai-docs paths.

output_examples.input: Prepare context for a BAML and MCP integration task.
output_examples.output: The agent identifies pages for BAML types, BAML functions, MCP tool servers, and MCP tool schemas, then produces a consolidated context brief.

output_examples.input: Decide whether to load full context for a migration.
output_examples.output: The agent compares targeted summaries with full-context loading, explains the expected token cost, and recommends the smallest context set that supports the migration.

best_practices: Start with index files and load page summaries only after identifying the relevant section.
Record source paths with each context summary so implementation decisions remain traceable.
Use web search only when local documentation is missing, stale, or explicitly insufficient.
anti_patterns: Loading full-context files for simple syntax or API questions.
Sending sub-agents to rediscover documentation that the parent agent already found.
Searching the web before checking available local ai-docs indexes.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown guidance skill that tells agents how to read local documentation files.

faq.question: What documentation format does it expect?
faq.answer: It expects an ai-docs structure with library indexes, page summaries, and optional full-context files.

faq.question: When should full-context files be loaded?
faq.answer: Use them for migrations, tutorials, architecture decisions, or first-time deep learning of a complex library.

faq.question: Can it use web search?
faq.answer: It recommends web search only after local docs are checked or when current information is required.

faq.question: How does it reduce token usage?
faq.answer: It favors small index files and targeted page summaries before loading large full-context files.

faq.question: Is it useful for sub-agents?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends preparing a concise context block and passing it to sub-agents before they start work.
